Event Name/Title

Trascendental Nectar

1990

Temple - SB class

Rita Aratori

1990

Source Audio

90-092-Transcendental Nectar (Mumbai)-SB_8_9_24_26-dt_90_10_16.mp3

Source Video

-

Verse

SB 08.09.24-25

Tags – Places

Demigod’s assembly

Tags – Pastimes

Mohini murti bewilders Rahu

Tags – Persons

Mohini murti, Rahu, Demigods

Tags – Themes

Amrta nectar’s power

Lecture Transcription

[SB 8.9.24]

"deva-linga-praticchannah

svarbhanur deva-samsadi

pravishtah somam apibac

candrarkabhyam ca sucitah"

TRANSLATION

Rahu, the demon who causes eclipses to the Sun and Moon, covered himself with the dress of a demigod, and thus entered the assembly of the demigods and drank nectar, without being detected by anyone, even by the Supreme Personality of Godhead. The Moon and the Sun, however, because of permanent animosity toward Rahu, understood the situation. Thus, Rahu was detected.

PURPORT by His Divine Grace A. C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada

The Supreme Personality of Godhead, Mohini-murti, was able to bewilder all the demons, but Rahu was so clever that he was not bewildered. Rahu could understand that Mohini-murti was cheating the demons, and therefore he changed his dress, disguised himself as a demigod, and sat down in the assembly of the demigods. Here one may ask why the Supreme Personality of Godhead could not detect Rahu. The reason is that the Lord wanted to show the effects of drinking nectar. This will be revealed in the following verses. The Moon and Sun, however, were always alert in regard to Rahu. Thus, when Rahu entered the assembly of the demigods, the Moon and Sun immediately detected him, and then the Supreme Personality of Godhead also became aware of him.

[SB 8.9.25]

“cakrena kshura-dharena

jahara pibatah sirah

haris tasya kabandhas tu

sudhayaplavito 'patat”

TRANSLATION

The Supreme Personality of Godhead, Hari, using His disc, which was a sharp like a razor, at once cut off Rahu's head. When Rahu's head was severed from his body, the body, being untouched by the nectar, could not survive.

PURPORT

When the Personality of Godhead, Mohini-murti, severed Rahu's head from his body, the head remained alive, although the body died. Rahu had been drinking the nectar through his mouth, and before the nectar entered his body, his head was cut off. Thus, Rahu's head remained alive, whereas the body died. This wonderful act performed by the Lord was meant to show that nectar is miraculous ambrosia.

Maharaj starts speaking here.

In everything that is revealed by the Supreme Personality of Godhead, when He comes into this world, there is a great lesson to be learned. The Supreme Personality of Godhead, who is perfect and complete in His own nature, as the supreme reservoir of all pleasure, has no need to enter into the stratum of this material existence. It is said: "karmanah daivenetrena". This means that the living being takes his birth in a particular species of life, according to one's previous pious or sinful actions, one's karma. Therefore, this material world, the wheel of samsara, we are forcibly being dragged from one body to another, birth after birth after birth, under the control of the laws of material nature. But Sri Krsna explains: “tribhir guna-mayair bhavair”, that [“I am not subject to the three modes of material nature”].

"janma karma ca me divyam

evam yo vetti tattvatah

tyaktva deham punar janma

naiti mam eti so 'rjuna"

That [“One who understands the transcendental nature of My appearance and activities in this world, such a person never takes birth again”]. Understand how divine and pure is the Lord's appearance in this world, that simply by understanding Him, we never have to take birth again in this world. The Lord comes to this world, as He explains in Gita:

"paritranaya sadhunam

vinasaya ca duskrtam

dharma-samsthapanarthaya

sambhavami yuge yuge"

[“To deliver the pious, to annihilate the miscreants and to reestablish the principles of religion”]. Therefore, in the divine lila that we are hearing from Srimad Bhagavatam, in fact all of the activities of the Lord and all His various divine incarnations, if we study and hear of the activities and the teachings from the sadhu, who was coming in the authorized sampradaya, then it will be revealed to us what the Lord is trying to reveal to us at this particular instant. Because, after all, everything He does in this world is only for the benefit, the upliftment of the conditioned souls who are suffering, for us.

Here in India, most everyone knows, basically, what are the pastimes of Sri Krsna, what are the pastimes of Sri Ramcandra. The pictures of their lila, we find even in the most ordinary buildings, even the children in school, they learn, to some extent. But, because people are not accepting the authorized process of understanding Krsna's lila, by hearing from the words of the authorized representative of God, therefore there are so many stories, so many pleasing stories. Recently, throughout India, especially Maharashtra, Bengal, there has been the celebration of the Navaratra, and, during these nine evenings, practically everywhere we find people with their sticks, performing the ras lila, dancing, and they know what is Krsna's lila and they’re all, as a social function, having a party celebrating Krsna's lila, but they do not understand what is this lila, or why. Therefore, it is simply so much social entertainment, without any purification or enlightenment. Therefore, even the subject matter of the divine activities of the Lord are not illuminating, enlightening, enrichening, unless we understand through the words of the sadhu, the Lord's representative. Therefore, Sri Krsna says:

"tad viddhi pranipatena

pariprasnena sevaya

upadeksyanti te jnanam

jnaninas tattva-darsinah"

[“If you want to understand the Truth, you must approach gurudev”], and Sri Krsna is the Absolute Truth.

Padma Purana says: "nama cintamanih krsnas caitanya ras vigraha", that means there is no difference between Krsna and His Name, Krsna and His divine Pastimes, Krsna and His transcendental Form. So, therefore, the Pastimes of the Lord, the Form of the Lord, the Name of the Lord are the Absolute Truth. Then Krsna says you cannot understand the Truth, unless you approach His representative. So, therefore, no one can enter into the fathomless storehouse of transcendental knowledge that is contained within the Pastimes of the Lord, unless they receive the key from the representatives of the Lord, who can open that divine storehouse.

The Pastimes of the Lord, the Names of the Lord without the direction of the saints, what we hear and what we chant is in fact only a shadow of the real substance. The real substance can only be revealed through the mercy of the vaisnava.

Therefore, we find people, because they take things cheaply, sahajiya means people take things cheaply. They want to enter into high understandings, without enquiring submissively and becoming the menial servant of a vaisnava.

Just like we find even amongst many of our own devotees, they read some book about some nitya siddha paramahamsas living in Vrndavan, who are having these beautiful darshans of Sri Sri Radha Syamsundar, and they’re this glorifying the holy abode of Vrndavan, and the devotees think: “Ah, what am I doing in this crazy place of Bombay? All this pollution, all these crazy children, all these women all around; what to speak of the temple president who always wants me to do some service… Why not just go to Vrndavan? It’s so nice. There’s so many brijwasis, there’s peacocks, there’s fresh air. It’s the holy abode. I will just go there. I’ll never have to work again. I can just beg for some food and just dance through the groves and chant “Jay Sri Radhe”. This is called sahajiya.

Krsna is not so cheap. You cannot see Vrndavan, simply by going there. You cannot understand Krsna's lila, you cannot even taste the sweetness of His Name, His real Name, unless you go through the authorized process that Krsna has explained. We should never try to imitate the paramahamsas. Yes, they are living in Vrndavan, but Krsna is coming to see them and they are transcendental to eating and sleeping. Raghunath das Goswami, Rupa Goswami, Sanatan Goswami these are the real residents of Vrndavan. They were so absorbed in Krsna's lila, they were so intensely weeping in separation, that they did not even consider the need to eat or to sleep. Simply for social fashion they would eat and sleep from time to time.

Are we on that level? Is Radha and Krsna coming because we’re not eating, they’re coming and bringing us nice prasada, personally? Ah?

There was one saint in Vrndavan. He used to do parikram of all brajadham every day. He would walk very fast. Usually, it takes forty days to do this parikram, but he would do it every day, and he would not eat one morsel of food until after he completed the parikram. So, you may think “So, I will do like that too”. But try to understand his consciousness. That one day he had long matted hair, because he did not care about his body. He just let, whatever happens to my body, let it be. So, one day, he was walking the sites and hills of Uchagaon, and there were very thorny bushes. In his matted locks of hairs got caught in the thorny bushes. So, he could not move. There was no one around. So, for hours and hours and hours and hours he was just trapped in these thorny bushes and there was no way out. So, then, what happened? Is Lord Krsna appeared and took his hair out of the thorny bushes. And he knew, Krsna knew, that he was there all day, now it had been night, he had not taken a drop of food or water. So, Krsna said he had some nice prasad. He said “You take this nice prasad”. The sadhu said “No, no, it’s not possible, I have to do my parikram of Vrndavan brajadham. I cannot take prasada until I complete it”. Krsna said “But, who are you doing it to please?” “I am the Supreme Personality of Godhead, I am Vrndavannath. I am Krsna”. “Which Krsna are you? If you’re Krsna from Dvaraka, if you’re Krsna from Mathura, are you Krsna from Vrndavan, how do I know which Krsna you are?” “I am Vrndavannath Krsna, Vrndavan”. This babaji said “Ah, how do I know you are Krsna? I will only know you are Krsna if Srimati Radharani Herself declares it, otherwise I cannot take your food”. So, at that time, Srimati Radhika appeared, and when he saw the lotus feet of Srimati Radharani, then the perfection of all his desires was fulfilled. And Srimati Radharani said “Yes, this is My Lord Sri Krsna”. And then babaji maharaj said “All right, then, I will take food from you. You have the authorization of Srimati Radharani”. So, is Radha and Krsna coming to bring food for you? Ah?

“maha jano yena gatah sa pantah": [“You can follow in the footsteps of the great saints, but don’t try to imitate them”]. How do they become such great saints? By hearing submissively from guru and being a menial servant. So, in another words, the Name of Krsna, the dham of Krsna, the Pastime of Krsna, we can only perceive a shadow, until Krsna reveals them to us. After all, in Vrndavan, there are also thousands of muslims who are living there, and they are killing innocent animals right in the streets and eating them. They have no faith in Krsna, some of them. By some inconceivable will of the Lord, they’ve been born in the holy dham, but they’re not Krsna conscious. There are even sinful sinful businessmen, who are coming to Mathura and Vrndavan to work in these big big oil refineries, and their only motive is greed and money. And they did not choose Vrndavan because it’s a holy place, they chose it because it’s on the Agra Delhi road, so good place for transporting and making lots and lots of money. So, there are thousands of such people who are interested in prostitutes, who are interested in drinking, and who are interested in money. Do you think they are staying in Vrndavan?

“premanjana-cchurita-bhakti-vilocanena”. [“You can only see Vrndavan when, by the grace of guru, your eyes are anointed with the pulp of love”]. So, we should not think that the Name of God, the Pastimes of the Lord and the holy dham of the Lord is so cheap, that we can simply go and enjoy, go and see, go and be purified. Srila Prabhupada said that “if you take the Lord so cheaply, to jump above your own position, you will not become purified, you will become putrefied”.

Maharaj speaks about how to get the understanding of naam, dham and lila of the Lord.

The process of understanding the truth of naam, dham and lila is the humble service of the Lord's devotees. Even Sri Krsna became the humble servant of Sandipani Muni. He was the Supreme Godhead Himself. This is when He was living in Mathura. He was not as a cowherd boy in this lila, but He was, He was Mathuranath, He was the prince, He was going to be King, but in the presence of His guru he went out in to forest to collect wood, the most menial service. It’s not the service of a pandit, it’s not the service of a king, it’s the service of a very very menial person, to just go in the jungles to find wood laying on the ground and to bring it back. But he was joyful, he was happy to do so, because this was guru seva. So, it is through the menial service of the great souls, by hearing from them submissively and by trying to please them by our sacrifice and life. This is the one and only process, by which the Lord is revealed to us. This is the only process, by which the Name of God, the lila of God and the dham of the Lord is revealed.

"tapo divyam putraka yena sattvam". [“Human life is meant for tapasya”]. Tapasya does not mean to do what we want. Even if you torture your body, even if you go to Badrinath and take ten baths a day, and eat only two drops of cow urine for a week, you will not see God by that process, unless it is the order of your gurudev. Real tapasya, if you’re asked to live in the city and eat halawa and go out and preach, that is the way you’ll see God. Ah? Because that is obedience, that is real sacrifice, that is humility.

Humility means “I do not know, I must approach God's representative and ask, what is that I should do”. Not that “Oh, I know what to do. I have read these books, I have seen, I know more than what you’re telling me. I have a higher understanding than what my guru maharaj is saying”. This cheap mentality is not the way to God. It is the way to enter deeply into our own vicious mind. And even such people, they may go to Vrndavan, and they may be roaming to the forest, saying “Oh, how blissful I am! You people, you devotees, look at what I was doing, what you’re doing recently. These dirty bathrooms and all the, you know, crazy people all around and such dirty mess and so much agitation of the mind. Now I’m simply Vrndavan, begging for food and living like the goswamis”. Are you living like the goswamis? Living like the goswamis is a deeply internal state of consciousness, it is not simply begging food, it is not simply living in a holy place. Living like goswamis means actually living in that holy place, actually seeing that place, actually experiencing, actually surrendering to the Lord. The six goswamis went to Vrndavan under the order of their guru, Sri Gauranga Mahaprabhu, they did not just go on their own.

So, there might be some peace, there might be some happiness, there might be some sublime thoughts of Krsna in the mind, but, the actual revelation of love cannot take place through that process. Do not take the dham so cheaply. The revelation of love, the actual higher realizations only come through surrender and sacrifice, by the mercy of the vaisnavas.

So, then, one may say “But there’s so many vaisnavas, I’ll just choose the one, that tells me what I like to do, and I’ll get his mercy”. That is not surrender. “After all, the vaisnavas here, they are telling me to sweep the floors, collect money, do sankirtan, give, go to these poor ghetto areas to give food to these low people. Uf! I could be with the paramahamsas in brajadham, dancing with the peacocks. Ah, why not? I’ll just find some sadhu who will say ‘Yes, do like this’.” That is not the process. You have guru, you must surrender to guru, that is the process. Not that you have a different guru for every mood. Ah? Every mood of every day you find a different guru. That is not guru. That is not disciple.

Krsna's sublime plan is to enquire submissively and render service, how can I best serve? Just like Lord Nityananda. How much he wanted to stay in Jagannath Puri with Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu? And Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu said “No, no. You cannot stay here with me; you go to Bengal and you preach. You spread the glories of the congregational chanting of the Holy Name to everyone without discrimination”. And he gave him a whole army of devotees to join him, and they were simply traveling from town to town, city to city, village to village, preaching the glories of the Lord, and it was not an easy thing. Sometimes they were persecuted, sometimes they were ridiculed, sometimes they were even physically abused. It would have been a lot easier just living in Puri, dancing with the Lord, but it was not the order of the Lord. Therefore, Viswanath Cakravarti Thakur has said:

"vyavasayatmika buddhir

ekeha kuru-nandana

bahu-sakha hy anantas ca

buddhayo 'vyavasayinam"

That [“If one wants to approach Krsna, one must be determined to make the order of guru his life and soul”]. That is our only real connection with Krsna; outside of the order of guru, any other conception of our connection with Krsna is only a shadow, it is not real, it is not the truth.

As we were explaining yesterday, Krsna cannot be manufactured by our tongue. It is not just by saying “Krsna”, that Krsna appears in our tongue, that is a material sound vibration. That Name of Krsna can only be revealed by the saint, by the sadhu, by the representative. When we hear from the guru, the Name enters into our ear, it goes into our heart, and then from our heart, if we are sincere, it rises to the platform of our tongue and manifests. So actually, it has been, by the causeless mercy of the sadhus, given to us. And, if we are sincere, from that heart it will dance upon our tongue. So, you can’t manufacture.

Similarly, just by going to Vrndavan or just by hearing the lilas of the Lord, it is not that you are entering into that spiritual stratum. You are just hearing, although, yes, there is some purification, but it is just the shadow of the real lila, it is the shadow of the real dham. But the real essence of the truth of Krsna in all of His various manifestations in this world can only be understood when He reveals them, and He reveals them from within our own heart when we become the humble servants of His servants. That is the only process, that is the sacrifice of human life. So, here, we are reading, this morning, about a wonderful lila which is incomprehensible to the atheist, because, first of all, we are talking about God, and God is He who knows past, present and future, who is omniscient and omnipresent. And here it is explaining that Rahu, he was sitting in the assembly of the demons, as Mohini murti bewildered every one of them, she is God Herself. Mohini murti was pouring the nectar in the mouth of each and every demigod and the demons were so infatuated by Her beauty and by Her charm, that they just sat there, very contently thinking that “If I gain Her gracious mercy, who needs the nectar? This is the higher nectar: Her love, Her charm, Her sweetness”. Even the demons are attracted by the sweetness of the Lord, but they do not understand, how to obtain it. Even Ravana, nothing of his whole golden kingdom, with all of his beautiful wives, attracted him after he saw the sweet beauty of Sri Sita. Krsna says in the Gita that “All that is beautiful and wonderful in this whole creation is only a tiny spark of My divine splendor”. Krsna is the source of all beauty, of all munificence, of all love, of all charm. He is Madan Mohan. He is the Attractor even of the attractor of all, in the form of Cupid. But Rahu, he was able to detect that “This is Krsna Himself, who has taken this form and who is cheating us, and all my cohorts, the other demons, they do not see it, but I understand that we are being cheated!”. So, he very cleverly disguised himself as a demigod, and sat amongst the demigods, and as Mohini murti was personally pouring the nectar in the mouth of each demigod, she did not detect that this is Rahu, the demon, and She poured within his mouth. How was it possible, if the Lord is omnipresent, omniscient, the Knower of everything, past, present and future? How She could not recognize that this is a demon? Ah? Even when the Lord, by His divine will, appears to forget, He is trying to teach us something. Nothing within the Srimad Bhagavatam is just a story. It is the divine lila, and in each and every syllable of the Bhagavatam all knowledge of the Absolute Truth is contained.

Bhakti Siddhanta Saraswati Thakur explained that “If you can understand in full, in reality even one syllable of one word of the Bhagavatam, you will be a fully enlightened soul”. What to speak of one Pastime? Mohini murti wanted to show us that She allows, the Supreme Personality of Godhead allows the demons, in order to fulfill their desires, to sometimes apparently, even out with the Lord Himself. How did Ravana steal Sita? By disguising himself as a saint, similar to Rahu. He disguised himself as a saint, just as Rahu disguised himself as a demigod, and he tricked Sita. She is the omniscient Mother of all creations. She knows everything, but She personally, temporarily forgot, by Her own sweet will, just so that She would be kidnapped by Ravana. And Ram, He knew that Maricha, that deer, was a demon. Why did He go chasing after it? He apparently was tricked by Maricha. He allowed Himself, He forgot that he was a demon, by His own sweet will. And said “Oh, very nice, you tricked me. I will go chase you”. It’s something very special to teach the world through this Pastime. But the Lord understands this is the difference between the activities of the conditioned soul and the lila of the Lord. The lila of the Lord is all orchestrated by His own sweet will, and just to make things interesting, and just to teach the world how to love Him and how to serve Him, sometimes He forgets, sometimes He appears that He is going to be defeated. So, the Lord wanted to show how He fulfills the desires even of the asuras where they can make their plans and even appear temporarily to trick the Lord to fulfill their own fastidious desires. But, in the end, they are always detected and destroyed. In the end, Ravana was killed. During the fight of Narasinghadev and Hiranyakasipu, at times Hiranyakasipu was actually winning the battle, but in the end, he was torn to pieces by the Lord. The Lord plays with the demons, just like He plays with His devotees in Goloka. He plays with the demons in this world. “Yes, yes, you are better than me, you think like that for some time”. So, what is happening here, is Rahu is disguising himself as a demigod. And in this way, he’s fulfilling his desires. Similarly, sometimes, demons disguise themselves as devotees. Demons will do anything to fulfill their own interests and the Lord will even allow them to get away with it, and He will even allow them to succeed for some time, but in the end, they are destroyed.

If you want to cheat, Krsna will let you be a cheater, but ultimately you will be cheated. So, we must not see things according to their superficial appearance. Ravana appeared to be a sadhu. Rahu appeared to be a demigod and even the Lord allowed him to go on like this. How was he detected? By the Sun God and the Moon God, who were great devotees of the Lord; they understood that this was their enemy who has disguised.

So, similarly, how do we know who is a real devotee? How do we know which book is actually good for us to read? How do we know which organization is the proper organization to make spiritual development? How do we know which group of people singing kirtan is actually purifying to my heart? Sometimes people with ulterior motives even appear to be devotees; and we cannot detect them, and the Lord lets them go on their way to fulfill their desires. It is only through the mercy of gurudev. It is only through the mercy of the great devotees of the Lord that we can understand who is who and what is what.

Therefore, although all the demigods and Krsna Himself were thinking: “Oh, this is a nice demigod, let us give him some nectar”, but the Sun and Moon God said: “No, he is a demon, he is Rahu”. Then he was detected. Just like in Vrndavan, when Prabhupada was building this temple, so many devotees were there and there were so many kirtans going on and so many prabachans and devotees were just going here and there everywhere and Prabhupada said: “No, no, you do not know, some of these people are not good people. They are sahajiyas, they are teaching something else. You will not be, you will not be purified but you will be confused, you will be contaminated”. How do we know who is who? You cannot know who is who. It is not possible for you to know who is who. They are reading all books. How do you know which book is actually a book of authorized knowledge, of real parampara? How do you know? You cannot know. You are a neophyte; you are not in a position to make such discriminatory decisions. That is why you have to have guru and you have to consult what is good, who is good, where is good. They can discriminate for you.

"acharya upasanam”. [“One who has guru, one who hears from acharya knows what is what”], otherwise we do not know, we cannot understand these things. Therefore, the safe path is to simply be humble. Guru Maharaj, representative of Guru Maharaj, what is best for me? I do not know. I am not going to meander and wonder into these higher realms of trying to decide these higher races of what will enlighten me; I’m simply your servant, you tell me. That is the safe, the straight and the real path, back home back to Godhead.

So, the Sun god and the Moon god, they detected that, in the guise of demigod, this man is cheating, and then Mohini murti took Her sudarsan cakra and cut off his head, and He had another lesson to teach us: the power of this nectar. That this ambrosial nectar, that was produced by Dhanvantri, had such potency, that, as that nectar was in his mouth, his head was cut off; because it touched his mouth, his head went on living. It was given great longevity of life and effulgence but the rest of his body, because the nectar had not reached that, immediately fell to the ground, dead. Mmh. And then, this head was given to Lord Brahma, and he made it into a planet. Ah, these things we will discuss, in our future mornings of Srimad Bhagavatam class.

But try to understand, ah, that even though he tasted this nectar, still his activities, even though he has long life, still his activities are envious of the Lord's devotees. Still, he’s an angry person, who does not have real peace, real tranquility, real love in his life. What does this mean? This means that the nectar of this world, however sweet it may be, will never satisfy your soul. The ultimate sense gratification of all creation is this nectar, and even that is not enough to give us peace or satisfaction. It is only the nectar of devotion. It is only when we dive into that bhakti rasamrta sindhu, that transcendental nectar of service to the Lord, that we can experience the real pleasure, which is the longing of the soul. So, we should long, we should cry, we should strive at every moment for that nectar, and that nectar is only obtainable by accepting the position of being the humble servant of the servant of the servant, in following the authorized path of devotional service to the Lord. Then the real sweetness of the divine lila, the divine Name and divine Form of God is fully manifested from within our heart. Then we can understand in truth the real beauty of Vrndavan, the infinite, eternal, spiritual grandeur of the top most spiritual planet, Goloka Vrndavan.

Q&A Transcription

  1. Question: not clear.

Answer: Therefore, if you are a doctor but you are not giving them Krsna Consciousness as well, you’re just giving cheap imitation of this nectar. Ah? Trying to give them some material strength, some longevity of life, good health. This nectar would give that beyond any medicine, just like the supreme material medicine, still no satisfaction. So, therefore, with every medicine you should give the Holy Name.

  1. Question: not clear.

Answer: If you have a pure heart, then you will humbly follow the direction of gurudev. You will not go, ah? And if you do, then it is under the direction of gurudev, then you will be protected, ah? But if it is not under his direction, then there is no protection. Then you will be misled. You will think that the ecstasies of devotion are something cheap, like some dramatic performance, some sentimentalism. Sometimes the great souls, they can even go to such places and they can, ah, just like there was one devotee, Krsna das babaji. He was a paramahamsa, one of Prabhupada's dear most God brothers. So, one time he was living in one of the Gaudiya math temples of Navadwip, and one of the acharya who was living there, he was very strict, according to the Rupa Sanatana line of divine thought. So, Krsna das babaji was always immersed in kirtan. He was a babaji. He was not a preacher so much. He was a babaji. But he was a real babaji, genuine lover of Krsna. So, he would always be in kirtan, and sometimes these sahaji groups they would be having kirtan and Krsna das babaji would be walking by, and he would go and join them, he was just tasting the Name everywhere he was. But he was living at this one Gaudiya math. So, the leader of the math said: “You should not be going to this sahajiyas. They are taking things cheaply, they are simply sentimentalists, crying, crying, crying without, but still attached to sense enjoyment. This is not real kirtan. We are sober, we are chanting and dancing, according to the way Sri Caitanya has taught us, not that we imitate him. But according to the line of great gurus”. So, it was found out that, although he was ninety-nine percent going to the nice Gaudiya kirtans, one percent he was still sometimes going with these other people. So, the acharya said “I cannot allow you to stay in our math. You are a paramahamsa, you are above of all these things, you can find Krsna anywhere; but these common devotees, if they see you going to these places, they will also think it’s all right and their life will be ruined. So, therefore, we have to have a standard in these temples, so you cannot stay here”. He was crying: “How can I tell you not to stay here? You are more advanced than me, but, for the sake of these devotees, I have to tell you that, if you continue like this, you cannot stay here”. So, he kept going, so, he was asked to leave. And after he left, the gurudev of that math and so many of his followers were crying and weeping because they had asked this man to leave, such a great saint, they were literally crying, but they had no choice. They had to protect those innocent devotees under them. So, one day Krsna das babaji was at the gate. Hare, Haribol, Haribol. He came back in. They were so glad to see him. He left for some days and he was back. “So, you are here now”. Gurudev said: “Ah, I told you that you should not live here”. He said, Krsna das babaji said: “Yes, you told me to leave, but you never told me I could not come back. So, I have not disobeyed you”. And then he didn’t go anymore. For the sake of the lesser devotees, he accepted this position of not going. So, you see, these great souls, we cannot imitate them. If we try to imitate them, it is like trying to imitate Lord Shiva. He drank poison, ah? If we try to drink that poison you will die. Ah?

The great souls they are in transcendental position; and, therefore, we must simply, those who are the real protectors of bhakti, who are the preachers of the Lord, the madhyama-adhikari. The uttama-adhikari, they do not even discriminate sometimes. They just Radhesyam. They are everywhere with Krsna. But we cannot be like them. Therefore, such uttama-adhikari, at times, come down to the position of the madhyama-adhikari, where they discriminate to teach us. A preacher must come down to the platform of a madhyama-adhikari. An uttama-adhikari, as long as he is staying on that position, he cannot preach. Gaurakisora das babaji, how advanced he was, when he would see people eating meat, when he would see people completely giving up all spiritual disciplines, not even chanting the Name of God, listening to cinema songs, he would think: “They’re so advanced, my God, they don’t even have to chant Hare Krsna. They can be with Krsna while eating meat and listening the cinema songs. But I am such a neophyte, if I don’t chant one-lakh of Names a day, I’ll forget Krsna”. Ah? This was his humility, and he was actually feeling like that. Now, if you think: “Well, so, if I want to be advanced, I should listen to cinema songs, eat meat and drink liquor. After all, that’s what Gaurakisora das babaji said”. Right? You would not be a preacher in that way. You would be an uttama-adhikari, who just remained on that transcendental position, who would just see everybody as God's eternal servant. But those who want to train people in the ordinary world, they have to come down to the level of saying “This is good, this is bad, this is sahajiya, this is real, this is sense gratification, this is devotional service”. They teach you how to discipline your life in this way. The disciple of Gaura, the only disciple of Gaurakisora das babaji was Bhakti Siddhanta Saraswati Thakur, who grasped the essence of Gaurakisora das babaji's divine love, and he taught very strictly the principles of bhakti rasamrita sindhu. So, it is by the mercy of such great souls that we can learn what is the path back home back to Godhead.

  1. Question: It is said that whether you know or you don't know you have to serve Krsna anyway. Rest part of question not clear.

Answer: Yes, that is the uttama-adhikari vision, but you do not benefit. If you do not know, there is no benefit. You are serving Krsna through His external energy. Therefore, you suffer. Ah? Krsna is reciprocating with everyone, according to how they approach Him. If you are serving Him in forgetfulness, in a sinful way, then you will get a very suffering reaction. That is also His lila. His lila is also to give pain to the sinners to gradually bring them to the platform of surrendering to His will. Mmh?

  1. Question: not clear.

Answer: There is nothing but Krsna. Maya is Krsna's energy. Krsna is non-different than His energy. Everything is Krsna. Everyone is serving Krsna. But some are serving Him favorably and some are serving Him unfavorably. Those who are serving Him favorably, get favorable result, those who are serving Him unfavorably, attain something unfavorable. Mmh? But it is all coming through Krsna and it is all going to Krsna.

  1. Question: not clear.

Answer: Independence. You see, He knows which demons want what, and He puts them right in the place where he can best use them. There’s still their independence, but He puts them where He can best use them for His purpose. He can use even the wicked, for His own purpose. Even in the material world, this is good management. Whenever a person's good points or bad points, a good manager understands how to use everything for the ultimate mission, right? Even if somebody who has very bad points, you figure out how these bad points could somehow or other serve the overall mission, and you put him in a place where he can function in that way. Right?

  1. Question: Pandavas are eternally liberated souls. Duryodhana... The rest part of the question is not clear.

Answer: In the Lord's personal lila, on a very higher level of understanding who these personalities are, sometimes it is very difficult. All we can understand is the lesson that Krsna is trying to teach us in this regard. Duryodhana, whatever he is, ultimately everyone is an eternally liberated soul. Correct? Are you not an eternally liberated soul? Every soul is eternally liberated. Some souls have forgotten it, and some souls have remembered it. Ah? So, yes, Duryodhana is an eternally liberated soul. So are you, and so am I, everyone is. But we have forgotten it, and he was very very deeply immersed in forgetfulness, when he was persecuting the Pandavas. Mmh? Now the question whether it is yoga maya or maha maya. Or it was by the misuse of independence, or by the divine will of the Lord, he just took an eternally liberated soul just to use him for a purpose, and makes him forget. But, actually, it’s a service to forget, or whether due to nitya-bandha, means one who, by misuse of independence, wants to forget, and Krsna gives him some forgetfulness. But in either case, the message is the same. Ah? The lesson is the same. That however great and powerful and learned you are, if you are not favorable to the Lord's devotees, you will lose everything and be destroyed in due course of time. Ah?

  1. Question: not clear.

Answer: Don't think about like and dislike. Just think about what gurudev likes, what the vaisnavas like. If they tell you to do something you love to do, then it’s your austerity to tolerate your loving to do it and do it, right? And if they tell you to do something you hate to do, with the same enthusiasm you have to tolerate your hatefulness and do it, cause you’re not doing it for you. A servant doesn't do it for himself. A servant does it for his master. We have to put aside our own feelings of like and dislike, difficult, easy. Mmh? This is all selfish thinking. This is not the thinking of a servant. This is the thinking of one who wants to be the master and the enjoyer. So, of course, we have these feelings, we cannot deny it. Therefore, we tolerate them, and we go on with our service, according to the direction of Krsna's representatives.

© Radhanath Swami Lecture Transcripts.
